ocr_mistral.text:
State of Kentucky County of Henderson SS.

I, James P. Breckinridge, Clerk of the County Court within and for the County and State aforesaid, do hereby certify that A. S. Park Esq., before whom the foregoing joint affidavit was made, and who has thereunto signed his name, was at the time of so doing a Notary in and for the County and State above named, duly commissioned, qualified and sworn; that all his official acts as such are entitled to full faith and credit, and that his said signature as it above appears is genuine. I further certify that, to the best of my knowledge and belief, the witnesses subscribing the foregoing affidavit are credible and of good standing in the community in which they reside. That I have no interest in this matter.

To certify all which I hereunto set my hand and the seal of my said Court at Office in Henderson this 21 day of August A.D. 1867

J. P. Breckinridge, Clerk
of Henderson County Court

---

SECTION.—To rate the Deputy Administrator the Clerk's Certificate, evidence may be required to show that the Deputy is duly appointed and authorized to have in open the Clerk's name and are the seal of the Court.—Therefore it is better to have the Clerk's own individual authorization of rates, and these rates the teacher and supervisor of a general authorization certificate respecting the Deputy which might be required if the papers were authenticated by him instead of the Clerk.

---

CERTIFICATE.
TO ALL WHOM IT MAY CONCERN.

Note.—This Certificate is to be signed by some one known to the Department of Washington, such as the Postmaster of the place where witnesses reside, or a member of Congress, or a United States Commissioner, or a Commissioner Officer of the Army.

It is hereby certified that the witnesses subscribing the foregoing joint affidavit are credible persons, and of good moral character, and reside as stated.

To certify which I hereunto set my hand at

in the State of

on this

day of

A. D. 186

---

INSTRUCTIONS.

1. "Clerk," "Town," "Corporation," or "County," as rates may be.
2. Name of the Soldier.
3. "Secretary," "Infantry," or "Artillery," "Volunteers of ___ Cents give state ___ manning the troops as indicated according to the arm of the service to which they belong. If at the regular service, in place of the foregoing, add: "United States Infantry," "Country," or "Artillery," as the rates may be.
4. Here give the issued period of known substitution of which affiants are cognizant.
5. "Walls (or" or "other discharged from," as the rates may be.
6. "Honorary Wounds," or the "Affiants of Typhoid Ploats," "Pneumonias," "Measles," "Chronic Diarrhea," "Dymatry," "Chokes Mariner," "Phillains," or any other disease, physical disability, or injury, whatever it may be, among it. It is very important to state correctly the cause of death, as it will be most accurately isolated upon by the Pension Office.

---

"Additional Evidence."

CLAIM FOR ARMY PENSION.

Act of A. D.
Mrs.
Widow of des'd
Late a in Co.
of the Regiment of

WITNESSES' AFFIDAVIT.

PRIOR OF

1. Widow's Identity.
2. Soldier's Identity.
3. Soldier's Service.
4. Cohabitation and Heteritly.
5. Soldier's Physical Condition prior to Enlistment.
6. Time, Place, and Cause of Death.
7. Continued Withdrawal of Applicant.

Case No.

EXTRACT.

Pension Office Circular, July 21, 1862.

"If the applicant claims a Pension at a widow of a deceased officer or soldier, she must prove the legality of her marriage, the death of her husband, and that she is still a widow."

...Affirmation.

State of Kentucky          County of Henderson   SS.  
  I, James P. Breckinridge             Clerk of the County       Court within and 
for the County and State aforesaid, do hereby certify that H. W. Cook          
Esq., before whom the foregoing joint affidavit was made, and who has thereunto signed his name, was at the time of so doing a 
Notary                   in and for the County and State above named, duly commissioned, qualified and sworn; that 
all his official acts as such are entitled to full faith and credit, and that his said signature as it above appears is genuine.  
I further certify that, to the best of my knowledge and belief, the witnesses subscribing the foregoing affidavit are credible 
and of good standing in the community in which they reside.  That I have no interest in the matter.  
To certify all which I hereunto set my hand and the seal of my said Court 
at Office in Henderson         this  
21st  day of August A.D. 1867.  
J. P. Breckinridge    Clerk, 
Henderson County      Court.


CERTIFICATE.  
TO ALL WHOM IT MAY CONCERN.  
  
It is hereby certified that the witnesses subscribing the foregoing joint affidavit are credible persons, and of good 
moral character, and reside as stated.  
  
To certify which I hereunto set my hand at ____________
  
in the State of ____________  on this 
_________ day of ____________A.D. 186__

INSTRUCTIONS.
1. The term “Degree of Dependency” or “Ceiling” as the case may be.
2. Decrease or Liability.
Note.—This Certificate is to be signed for one year and beyond in the Department at Washington, usually by the Department of the place where the witness resides or a member of Congress, 
or by a United States Commissioner or a United States Marshal of the District.

"Additional Evidence."

7. Regimental Code of Applicants.
